mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during industrial production contractions with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, high borrowing costs constrain corporate leverage and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 65% of periods where the federal funds rate remains above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while consumer price inflation accelerates creates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample R-squared or fails to predict negative 5-day returns with directional accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening economic output that lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during broad economic expansions.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of expansionary periods marked by rising industrial production.
